message:
perf: Add thread-safe model cache to prevent double loading

Problem: Model was loaded twice during inference - once in main init,
and again in autoDetectLoraAdapter() just to compute model hash.
This wasted ~5GB memory and doubled startup time.

Solution:
- Add thread-safe model cache in llama.zig with mutex protection
- Cache uses (path_hash, gpu_layers) as key for deduplication
- Reference counting ensures safe cleanup when ref_count reaches 0
- Change autoDetectLoraAdapter() to accept model_hash instead of
  loading model again

Results:
- Before: 2 model loads, ~5GB wasted memory
- After: 1 model load, optimal memory usage
- Performance maintained at ~46 tok/s on M2 Pro

files.patch:
@@ -211,7 +211,29 @@ if (spec_pct > 0.40) return .speculation;
 - `src/inference_profiler.zig` - New pprof-style profiler
 - `src/optimizer.zig` - Fixed layer count heuristics (hardcoded → actual from llama.cpp)
 - `src/scheduler.zig` - Integrated profiler with ZELL_PROFILE=1 flag
-- `src/main.zig` - Documented speculation dead end with benchmark data
+- `src/main.zig` - Documented speculation dead end with benchmark data; Fixed double model load in autoDetectLoraAdapter
+- `src/llama.zig` - Added thread-safe model cache to prevent double loading
+
+## Part 5: Double Model Loading Fix
+
+### Problem
+Model was being loaded twice during inference:
+1. First load: Main model initialization (12123 MiB free)
+2. Second load: `autoDetectLoraAdapter()` was loading the model again just to get its hash (7623 MiB free - 5GB wasted!)
+
+### Root Cause
+`autoDetectLoraAdapter()` in main.zig was calling `llama.Model.load()` to compute the model hash for auto-trained LoRA adapter lookup.
+
+### Solution
+1. **Removed redundant model load** from `autoDetectLoraAdapter()` - now accepts model hash as parameter
+2. **Added thread-safe model cache** in llama.zig with:
+   - Mutex-protected global cache (up to 4 models)
+   - Reference counting for safe cleanup
+   - Automatic deduplication based on path hash + GPU layers
+
+### Results
+- **Before**: 2 model loads, ~5GB wasted memory, doubled startup time
+- **After**: 1 model load, optimal memory usage, faster startup
 
 ## Benchmark Scripts
